title: Mid-50s, on my way to $1M — hoping to help others before I retire
author: WillingnessNo57
selftext:
I’m in my mid-50s and still on this journey.

This year has been a strong one so far — up about $384K YTD, and currently sitting around $1.5M. Grateful for the progress, but I know the market can humble anyone at any time.

I didn’t start with a perfect plan. Like many here, I made mistakes along the way, learned the hard way about risk, and had to rebuild discipline over time.

At this stage in my life, it’s not just about hitting a number anymore. Of course, I’d like to keep growing this portfolio and stay consistent — but more importantly, I hope that before I retire, I can use what I’ve learned to help others who are still figuring things out.

Whether it’s sharing ideas, avoiding mistakes, or just giving perspective — that part matters more to me now.

Still learning. Still improving. Still in the race.
